title: 🧅 How to regrow Green Onions
steps: Leave about an inch of the white root intact.
Place them root-down in a small glass or jar with just enough water to cover the roots.
Place on a sunny windowsill and change the water every 2-3 days.
outro: *In about a week, you'll have brand new green shoots ready to harvest!*

title: 🥔 How to plant sprouting Potatoes
steps: Cut the potato into 2-inch chunks, ensuring every chunk has at least one 'eye'.
Leave the chunks on the counter for 24-48 hours so the cut sides dry out and form a callous (this prevents rotting).
Plant them in a deep pot or garden soil with the 'eye' facing upwards, covered by 3 inches of soil.
outro: *Keep the soil moist, and soon a whole new potato plant will emerge!*

title: 🥬 How to regrow Celery
intro: **The base of a celery bunch is a biological powerhouse.**
steps: Cut off the bottom 2 inches of the celery bunch.
Place it base-down in a shallow bowl of water (about an inch deep).
Keep it in a sunny spot and change the water every couple of days.
After a week, leaves will sprout from the center. You can then transfer it to soil!
outro: *Free organic celery directly from your kitchen scraps!*

title: 🥗 How to regrow leafy greens
intro: **You can regrow the heart of almost any clumped leafy green!**
steps: Chop off the bottom 1 to 2 inches of the lettuce head.
Place the stump in a shallow dish with a half-inch of water.
Change the water daily. Within 3-4 days, you will see roots growing from the bottom and new leaves from the top.
Once established, plant the stump in soil, leaving only the new leaves exposed.
outro: *Perfect for a perpetual supply of fresh salad leaves!*

title: 🧄 How to grow Garlic Sprouts
intro: **Did a clove of garlic sprout a green shoot in your pantry?**
steps: Instead of throwing it away, place the sprouted clove in a small cup with just a splash of water at the bottom.
The green shoot will grow rapidly in a sunny spot.
Snip the green shoots off with scissors and use them exactly like green onions or chives!
outro: *Garlic sprouts have a mild, delicious garlic flavor perfect for garnishes.*
